tool.name: github_add_review_to_pr
tool.title: add_review_to_pr
tool.description: Add a review to a GitHub pull request. review is required for REQUEST_CHANGES and COMMENT events.

position.description: The position in the diff where you want to add a review comment. Note this value is not the same as the line number in the file. The position value equals the number of lines down from the first "@@" hunk header in the file you want to add a comment. The line just below the "@@" line is position 1, the next line is position 2, and so on. The position in the diff continues to increase through lines of whitespace and additional hunks until the beginning of a new file.
position.title: Position

pr_number.description: Pull request number in the repository.
pr_number.title: Pr Number

review.description: Review body to submit. Required when requesting changes or leaving a comment.
review.title: Review

action.description: Review action to take. Defaults to `COMMENT` when omitted.

file_comments.description: Optional inline file comments to include with the review.
file_comments.title: File Comments

commit_id.description: Optional commit SHA to anchor the review.
commit_id.title: Commit Id

tool.name: github_get_profile
tool.title: get_profile
tool.description: Retrieve the GitHub profile for the authenticated user.

result.title: Profile
result.description:
Many connectors (especially OAuth-based) act on behalf of a user.
This class represents the user's profile, which is typically retrieved from
the connector's API during the OAuth flow.

Implementation details for different connectors vary, so we can't guarantee
that all fields will be present.

tool.name: github_search
tool.title: search
tool.description: Search files within a specific GitHub repository. Provide a plain string query, avoid GitHub query flags such as ``is:pr``. Include keywords that match file names, functions, or error messages. ``repository_name`` or ``org`` can narrow the search scope. Example: ``query="tokenizer bug" repository_name="tiktoken"``. ``topn`` is the number of results to return. No results are returned if the query is empty.

topn.description: Maximum number of results to return.
topn.title: Topn

repository_name.description: Repository or repositories to search within. Use this to narrow the search scope.
repository_name.title: Repository Name

org.description: Optional GitHub organization to scope the search.
org.title: Org

tool.title: search_prs
tool.description: Search GitHub pull requests.

repo.description: Repository or repositories to search within. Use `owner/name` strings.
repo.title: Repo

org.description: Optional GitHub organization to scope the search.
org.title: Org

topn.description: Maximum number of results to return.
topn.title: Topn

tool.name: github_search_repositories
tool.title: search_repositories
tool.description: Search for a repository (not a file) by name or description. To search for a file, use `search`.

per_page.description: Maximum number of results to return.
per_page.title: Per Page

org.description: Optional GitHub organization to scope the search.
org.title: Org

topn.description: Alias for `per_page` used by some callers.
topn.title: Topn
